description: Baseline. 3/3 solved but 2/3 timed out at 120s before `(turn-answer! …)`, so their iter counts are unknown and got penalty 30 each. Real signal we have: task 5 needed 12 iterations and ~$0.030. Score is iter-count-sum, lower is better.

asi.hypothesis: establish baseline
asi.primary_metric: iter_score = sum across tasks of (iterations OR FAIL_PENALTY=30 if no iter count)
asi.key_problem: 2/3 tasks hit 120s timeout. GLM-5.1 burns many iterations on slurp/spit/patch mistakes before turn-answer!. Next: count iters from trace stream so timeouts don't blind us; raise per-task timeout to 240s.
user_concerns_added: antifit: revalidate every keep on a different rotation slice
metric_clarity: turns×iters sum example (3+5=8) matches iter_score
context_optimization: include mean_prompt_chars + total_tokens; treat context inflation >50% as keep-blocker

description: Mixed workload baseline (2 × 4Clojure + 1 × filewrite). iter_score=20 across 3 tasks. mean_iter_pass=6.67, max=12. No timeouts. Established new bar with block_errors / z_patch_share / ext_prompt_chars metrics now wired in.

asi.hypothesis: establish mixed-workload baseline
asi.headroom_for_optim: foundation_prompt_chars=8303 dominates; system 3429 next; user 1390 is irreducible without help. Cutting the foundation/z extension prompts is the biggest context lever.
next_targets: shrink foundation `:ext/prompt` body without breaking existing tool dispatch / tests
shrink CORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT (gate language is verbose; can compress)
reduce block_errors by guiding model to v/patch over slurp/spit in user prompt
encourage z/patch over v/patch when editing .clj files (z_patch_share metric tracks this)
asi.block_errors_baseline: not measured before this commit; runner now captures it. Re-baselining will set the bar.
asi.z_patch_share_baseline: not measured; baseline metric files don't have it yet because run preceded the metric. First post-commit run sets it.

description: Fixed-workload baseline. Same 3 tasks every iter (4Clojure #5, #15, filewrite fw-005). iter_score=39 (no timeouts; pass_count=3). This is the first run where iter_score is actually comparable across iterations.

asi.hypothesis: anchor a comparable baseline with FIXED_WORKLOAD
key_findings: #15 'Double Down' now takes 23 iters — unexpectedly high; model loops on the '__' textual substitution
v_patch_calls=3, z_patch_calls=0 — model uses v/patch exclusively; z/patch share metric currently 0 even on .clj edits
block_errors=4 — reduced from the rotating-workload baseline (9); the fixed tasks happen to be cleaner shapes for the model
ext_prompt_chars=12410 is the dominant context floor; user_prompt_mean=1409
next_targets: lower svar TTFT/idle to fail fast on stuck provider calls (will pair with the next run)
tighten foundation/z extension prompts to shrink ext_prompt_chars
audit gate language in CORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT for compressibility

description: svar TTFT=10s + idle=10s + dropped virtual threads. pass dropped 3 → 2 (task 4Clojure #15 failed at iter 1 with svar.core/http-error — TTFT fired during legitimate reasoning startup). iter_score 39 → 59, block_errors 4 → 6.

asi.hypothesis: 10s TTFT/idle would fail fast on stuck calls without breaking real ones
asi.result: regression: 10s caught real reasoning startups, not just hangs

per_task.4clojure-15: FAIL 1 iter 22s wall - TTFT/idle fired during reasoning
per_task.filewrite-fw-005: PASS 19 iters 173s (was 4 iters 60s - 5x worse, likely many retries on transient timeouts)

asi.rollback_reason: pass_count dropped, iter_score got worse on the same fixed workload
asi.next_action_hint: Bump svar TTFT to 20-30s and idle to 30-45s. glm-5.1 cold-start headers sometimes >10s; legitimate reasoning streams can pause briefly. Virtual-thread removal stays (orthogonal).
asi.svar_changes_to_partial_revert: TTFT 10000 -> 30000, idle 10000 -> 45000; keep cached thread pool. Will apply in ../svar manually since vis discard doesn't touch the sibling repo.

description: svar TTFT 90s→30s, idle 120s→45s, virtual threads → cached daemon pool. iter_score 39 → 33 (-15%), pass_count 3, block_errors 4 → 2, cost -7%, tokens -14%. The TTFT/idle tightening clears genuine stalls fast without breaking real reasoning queues.

asi.hypothesis: 30s TTFT + 45s idle catches real hangs without breaking glm-5.1 reasoning cold starts
asi.noise_floor_evidence: individual tasks moved both ways; total improved. Confirms LLM stochasticity is non-trivial on this workload.
next_steps: run revalidate.sh on rotating offsets to confirm svar tweak generalizes beyond fixed-3 tasks
after that: try shrinking foundation_prompt_chars (8303) — the biggest remaining context lever
asi.kept_files: ../svar HEAD now at 942ee16e (autoresearch branch ecdd09c2 unchanged)

description: Confirmation run, same code as previous keep. iter_score 33 → 27, block_errors 2 → 0, all 3 pass, wall 276s → 176s. Reproduces the svar TTFT/idle tightening win and bounds the LLM stochasticity (per-task variance: #15 8↔5, #5 17↔11, fw-005 8↔11).

asi.hypothesis: re-run same fixed workload + same code to confirm svar tweak is real, not noise

noise_floor_per_task_iters.4clojure-15: 23, 8, 5 -> range 18, but the 23 was a stall pre-svar-fix

asi.interpretation: After the svar fix, the visible noise floor on this 3-task workload is ~±6 iter_score points. The 39 → 27 swing (−12) sits at ~2x noise — a real effect even if not every task improves.
next_targets: revalidate on rotated workload (./autoresearch.revalidate.sh) for anti-overfit
then tackle foundation_prompt_chars (still 8303 — biggest remaining lever)

description: Preflight gate fix + evidence-prior tightening. iter_score 27 → 11 (-59%). Pass 3/3 maintained; block_errors 0 → 2 (within +1 threshold); mean_iter_pass 3.67. Per task: 4Clojure-5=4 iters (was 11), 4Clojure-15=2 (was 5), fw-005=5 (was 11). Root cause: final-answer preflight gate was calling final-answer-gate-error with no previous-iterations, so every clean-answer-only iteration was falsely rejected with "no evidence for this turn yet" even when prior iterations had real work.

asi.hypothesis: preflight final-answer gate was rejecting legitimate answer-only iterations because previous-iterations was not threaded through
asi.root_cause: loop.clj:1578 called final-answer-gate-error environment iteration-position [] nil with no extra-ctx; gate evidence-prior? defaulted to (boolean (seq nil)) = false even after multiple successful probe iters
asi.secondary_fix: evidence-prior? now requires a prior iteration to contain at least one block with nil :error AND not a turn-answer call; closes loophole where preflight-rejected-only iters would falsely count as evidence

description: Confirmation re-run. iter_score 11 → 7. Pass 3/3, block_errors 0. mean_iter_pass=2.33, max=3. Reproduces preflight-gate fix; gap from baseline (27→7) is ~4× the prior noise floor (±6). All tasks now near the structural minimum.

asi.hypothesis: re-run same code to confirm 27 -> 11 was not LLM noise
asi.result: confirmed and improved further this run (LLM variance pulled in our favor)
asi.interpretation: structural floor for these 3 tasks is around 2-3 iters each. Pre-fix the floor was inflated by spurious gate refusals. Post-fix, the floor sits at iter_score~7-11 depending on LLM noise.
next_targets: try shrinking system_prompt_chars (3429) gate language now that the gate semantics are clearer
or shrink user prompt + foundation editing prompt (combined 3000+ chars)

description: Compressed CORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT 3429 → 2512 chars (-27%). ext_prompt_chars 6104 → 5187 (-15%); context_score 7513 → 6596 (-12%). iter_score ties at 7 (the post-fix floor for this workload). pass_count 3, block_errors 0. The 11-then-7 sequence on the same prompt confirms LLM noise floor; the smaller prompt costs less without hurting iter count.

asi.hypothesis: shrink core system prompt to lower context floor without hurting iter score
next_steps: tackle foundation editing prompt (1067 chars of the 1997 foundation)
or shrink user prompt template (~1500 chars per task)

description: Edit-prompt shrink (1067→762 chars): block_errors 0→6 violates keep-blocker (+1). Iter_score 7→10 also worse. fw-005 spent 6 iters with 6 block_errors — likely the trimmed wording (esp. removed "Bind windows, not whole files" + Nippy detail) lost a hint the model relied on.

asi.hypothesis: tighten editing prompt
asi.next_action_hint: the editing prompt cut probably removed too much; keep z/patch + v/patch hints; restore Nippy-window guidance or merge into v/cat docstring
asi.system_prompt_chars_at_revert: expected to revert to 2512 + 1997 foundation; runner cache will refresh on next change

description: User-prompt shrink (4Clojure 1195→656, filewrite 828→415): iter_score 7→20 — 4Clojure #15 ballooned 2→13 iters. Hint loss matters: removing "Some blanks need multiple forms (for example `:a :b :c` to satisfy `(list __)`)" likely cost the model. Pass kept 3/3, block_errors only 1, but primary regressed badly. Discard.

asi.hypothesis: shrink user prompt by dropping Restrictions section already covered in core prompt
asi.next_action_hint: keep the 'multiple forms (e.g. :a :b :c)' nudge in the 4Clojure prompt; that is the hint the model needs for blank-substitution problems
asi.ext_prompt_chars_at_run: shows 4882 because the prompt-size cache key was using src.name and conflated multiple core.clj files (now fixed in runner.py via relative path key)
asi.noise_floor_widening: this run shows 13 iter ceiling per task is reachable with the wrong prompt; pre-fix range 7-11

description: Add minimal-shape hint to LOOP DISCIPLINE: iter_score 7→14 — worse. Hypothesis was the model would adopt 2-iter shape; instead it took fw-005=8, 4clojure-5=4, 4clojure-15=2. Net regression. Prompt size jumped 2512→2710. Sign that the hint either confused or was just noise. Discard.

asi.hypothesis: explicit '2-iter minimal shape' hint nudges model toward optimal
asi.rollback_reason: iter_score 7 -> 14 regression; +198 prompt chars for no gain
asi.next_action_hint: either the hint is wrong content or the model already knows; try a different lever: improve answer-with-extension preflight error message to push model toward the 2-iter shape on the FIRST mistake instead of via prompt

description: Reword answer-with-extension preflight error: iter_score 7→10 worse, block_errors 0→2. No win. Pivoting: move strategy into per-extension prompts; teach model structural editing exists in z/.

asi.hypothesis: clearer preflight rejection text reduces iters
asi.pivot: user feedback: strategy belongs in extension prompts (z/ should say 'I am STRUCTURAL EDITING for Clojure'); system prompt should teach approach (read-runtime-truth, probe-before-patch) not list gates

description: Refactor z/ ext prompt to announce STRUCTURAL EDITING + workflow. iter_score 7→12; z_patch_share still 0%. Conclusion: z/ prompt content is fine but user-prompt templates hard-code `(v/patch [...])` examples — that example dominates regardless of extension prompt. Pivot: combine z/ prompt rewrite + user-prompt template update in one experiment so the model has both axes pointing at z/patch.

asi.hypothesis: loud z/ prompt drives z_patch_share up; structural editing should be easier on EDN/.clj than text v/patch
asi.rollback_reason: primary 7->12 regression; z_patch_share unchanged at 0% — the prompt change was the right shape but didn't win the example battle in user prompts
asi.key_insight: user prompts contain a hard-coded `(v/patch ...)` example block. that explicit example outweighs whatever the extension prompts say. need to change user prompts too OR make the extension prompt include a working z/patch example with the exact shape models will copy.
asi.next_action_hint: re-apply z/ prompt rewrite + update 4Clojure user prompt to use z/patch shape on solution.edn (it's an EDN file — structural editing is the natural fit). filewrite tasks may keep both options since starter files vary.

description: Prompt example `:replace 'YOUR_ANSWER` is a trap: SCI evaluates the quote, z/patch sees the unquoted list, writes `(1 2 3 4)` instead of `'(1 2 3 4)`. Judge does textual __ substitution → `(1 2 3 4)` is a fn call (1 not callable) → ClassCastException Long→IFn. Confirmed via /tmp REPL test of patch-safe with three :replace shapes. iter_score 7→41 — bad. Next: rewrite prompt to use (z/source "...") so reader macros survive.

asi.hypothesis: quoting :search and :replace fixes 4clojure-5
asi.rollback_reason: SCI eval consumes the quote before z/patch sees it; file ends up without the quote; judge's textual __ substitution then evaluates `(1 2 3 4)` as fn call. confirmed via /tmp/z-test patch-safe REPL: :replace '(1 2 3 4) wrote `(1 2 3 4)`; :replace (z/source "'(1 2 3 4)") wrote `'(1 2 3 4)`.
asi.next_action_hint: update 4clojure prompt: `:replace (z/source "YOUR_ANSWER")` so reader macros (quote, syntax-quote, #(), @, ^) survive. Or revert to v/patch for the sentinel-replacement case (it's textual, not structural).

description: All 3 pass; 4Clojure-5 now PASS @ 2 iters (was FAIL with quote-strip bug). iter_score 7→10 within post-fix noise. BUT ext_prompt_chars 5187→6390 (+23%) violates +5% keep-blocker. z/ prompt grew too verbose. Need to compress while preserving the SCI-strips-reader-macros warning and z/source guidance.

asi.hypothesis: z/source-explicit prompt fixes 4Clojure-5
asi.result: correctness restored (3/3 pass, 4cloj-5 from FAIL to PASS@2-iter)
asi.next_action_hint: compress z/ prompt: keep the SCI-strips-reader-macros warning + (z/source) shape + workflow but cut redundant phrasing. target z_prompt_chars <= ~1000 (was 678 originally, kept-best uses 678).

description:
Combo win after deep trace investigation. iter_score 7→6 (NEW BEST). All 3 tasks at 2 iters each = floor. Pass 3/3. Block_errors 0. ext_prompt_chars 5187→5241 (+1.04%, under blocker).

Changes:
1. ENGINE: answer-with-extension preflight no longer rejects whole iteration — drops only the turn-answer form and lets extension calls EXECUTE. Stops the "model keeps re-emitting same bundle because its z/patch never lands" loop (saw 5-iter loop in failed run).
2. PROMPT: z/ extension prompt now warns ":search/:replace are values at SCI boundary; reader macros consumed; use (z/source \"...\") to preserve quote/#()/@/^". This fixed 4Clojure-5 quote-strip bug (FAIL→PASS).
3. 4Clojure user prompt uses z/patch + (z/source) shape with literal-list example.
4. Runner cache: relative-path fingerprint key (multiple core.clj no longer collide).

Note: z_patch_share=0 reads as a metric bug — z/patch was called inside (do ...), and the trace-event hook only fires for bare top-level tool calls. Solution files confirm z/patch ran (diff shows __SENTINEL → '(1 2 3 4) etc.).

asi.hypothesis: engine bundling fix + prompt explicitly teaches (z/source) escape hatch
asi.primary_metric_delta: 7 -> 6 (-14%), and ALL three tasks now at 2-iter floor
asi.key_engine_change: answer-with-extension preflight now drops only the turn-answer entry; extension calls execute; previously the entire iteration was rejected and the model looped because its work never landed in the journal
asi.key_prompt_change: (z/source "...") usage taught both in z/ ext prompt and 4Clojure user prompt; solves the SCI-consumes-reader-macros trap that was making :replace '(1 2 3 4) write `(1 2 3 4)` and break the judge
asi.metric_bug_noted: z_patch_share reads 0% because trace tool-event only fires for bare tool calls, not ones wrapped in (do ...); the solution files confirm z/patch actually ran
next_ideas: fix metric parser to count tool calls in any (do ...) wrapper
revalidate.sh on rotated workload for anti-overfit
we are now at the structural minimum for these 3 tasks; further wins likely require different workload

description: Confirmation re-run. iter_score=6 reproduced. All 3 tasks at 2-iter floor. z_patch_share=100% this run (vs 0% prior — depends on whether model wraps z/patch in `do` or emits bare). Wall 47.92s — fastest yet. Result is stable; we've hit the structural floor for this 3-task workload (probe-iter + answer-iter = 2 iters minimum).

asi.hypothesis: re-run same code; iter_score=6 is the floor not a fluke
asi.iter_score_floor_evidence: for 3 tasks needing >=1 patch each, structural minimum is 3 * 2 = 6 (probe-iter + answer-iter); we now hit it consistently
asi.noise_floor: with engine bundling fix, per-task variance has collapsed: 2 iters each, both runs identical
next_ideas: fix the z_patch_calls metric to count tool calls inside (do ...)
consider whether 1-iter answer is possible (would require gate change: bundle probe+answer into single iter via journal-render-after-each-form)

description: filewrite prompt shrink (827→410 chars) regressed fw-005 from 2 iters to 4. Trace shows model emitted z/patch WITHOUT `:path` (the cut removed all example shapes). 2 block_errors from missing :path; model eventually switched to v/patch and got it. Pass 3/3 but iter_score 6→8 and block_errors 0→2 (keep-blocker violated).

asi.hypothesis: filewrite prompt didn't need v/patch example
asi.next_action_hint: the model needs at least one example with :path; the z/ ext prompt shows usage but not with :path in the inline shape. Add a minimal `(z/patch [{:path "foo.clj" ...}])` line to the z/ ext prompt itself so any caller has the canonical shape visible.

description: iter_score=6 tied; pass 3/3; block_errors 0. Adds explicit :path-required edit-map shape to z/ prompt (defensive fix for the bug seen last run). BUT primary unchanged, ext_prompt_chars +4.5% eats the +5% margin. Per strict rule (keep only on primary improvement) discard. Defensive shape can be reintroduced if a future fw-005-type failure recurs.

asi.hypothesis: explicit :path-required shape in z/ prompt
asi.result: tied at 6; safer but didn't move primary; ate margin
asi.next_action_hint: lever: shrink elsewhere. cost_usd is still 0.018 (~$0.02/run); total_tokens 21k; mean_prompt 1653. tackle ANSWER_IR block of system prompt (~250 chars enumerating all hiccup tags) -- not needed for these tasks since answer is always [:ir [:p "solved"]]; could be deferred to channel hook only when answer parse fails.

description: EMIT_FINAL prompt update (truer to engine behavior, -63 chars). iter_score 6→7. fw-005 added a v/cat verify step in iter 2 (LLM noise or new prompt nudged toward caution). Pass 3/3, block_errors 0, secondaries fine but primary unchanged-or-worse. Discard.

asi.hypothesis: tighter EMIT_FINAL section (no behavioral change, just less stale wording)
asi.rollback_reason: primary 6 -> 7 (fw-005 added a v/cat verify); within noise but rules say discard on no-improvement
asi.observation: we're at the workload floor. multiple discards in a row confirm the noise floor sits at 6-7 with this fixed workload. Further structural gains need either (a) gate loosening to allow 1-iter bundles or (b) a different/harder workload to expose more structure.
asi.next_strategy: switch to anti-overfit/generalization work: run revalidate rotation to broaden the signal, and write up findings to autoresearch.ideas.md so the loop has a stronger non-overfit target

description: Gate loosening alone (allow `(do ext-call+ (turn-answer! literal-IR))`) does not exercise on the workload — model didn't discover the new shape. Tied at 6. Pass 3/3, block_errors 0, no regression. Re-applying together with a prompt nudge in next experiment.

asi.hypothesis: gate loosening alone unlocks 1-iter answers
asi.rollback_reason: primary tied (model didn't try the shape); rule says discard on no improvement
asi.next_action_hint: combine: gate loosen + one-line nudge in EMIT_FINAL ('Exception: (do <ext-call> (turn-answer! literal-IR)) is allowed when IR has no free symbols').

description: Bundle shortcut + nudge: block_errors 0→7 catastrophic. Model tried bundles but botched edit shapes. Pivoting on user feedback: (1) check Codex exact patch format via Exa, (2) strip extension-specific mentions from core prompt (currently CORE references v/patch, z/patch, exa — wrong layering, extensions should own their tools).

asi.hypothesis: shortcut bundle + prompt nudge yields 1-iter answers
asi.next_action_hint: user direction: research Codex apply_patch format on Exa, then refactor core prompt to not mention specific extensions (v/, z/, exa/) — those belong in extension prompts

description:
LAYERING REFACTOR per user direction: CORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT no longer names specific extension tools. Removed mentions of v/cat, v/patch, z/patch, exa/web-search. Core now describes the harness generically: "Tools : every tool comes from an active extension (see <extensions>); the core harness names none." Each extension owns its own surface in its own prompt.

iter_score=6 tied with best. pass 3/3, block_errors 0, z_patch_share 100%, ext_prompt_chars 5241→5232 (-0.2%). This is a correctness/layering improvement requested by the user (extensions own their surfaces), not a metric win — but it does not regress.

asi.hypothesis: removing extension-specific names from CORE shouldn't regress; extensions own their tools
asi.result: tied at iter_score=6; layering is now correct
asi.user_direction_addressed: CORE prompt no longer says 'v/cat, v/patch, z/patch, exa/web-search'; just refers to <extensions> generically
asi.codex_format_research: Codex apply_patch uses *** Begin Patch / *** Update File / @@ class / +/- lines format. Vis has its own v/patch + z/patch tooling that diverges; switching to Codex's text-diff format would require new parsers and lose the structural z/patch advantage. Out of scope this iteration.
next_targets: audit foundation v/ editing prompt for redundancy with z/
consider adding a single SCI quote-strip warning to z/ prompt only (don't duplicate in 4Clojure user prompt)

description: Confirmation re-run after layering refactor. iter_score 6→7, fw-005 went 2→3 iters with 1 block_error. Within post-fix noise (6-7 range observed across multiple runs). pass 3/3. Confirms code is stable; 6 is best-case, 7 is typical when fw-005 adds a verify step.

asi.hypothesis: confirm layering refactor is stable
asi.result: stable within noise; per-task variance dominated by fw-005 (sometimes 2 iters, sometimes 3 with a verify v/cat)

description: Second confirmation: iter_score=6 reproduced with layering refactor in place. pass 3/3, block_errors 0, z_patch_share 100%, wall 49.42s. Per-task all at 2 iters. Stable floor confirmed across runs (sequence: 6, 6, 7, 6 on identical code = noise floor ~±1).

asi.hypothesis: iter_score=6 is the genuine floor for this 3-task fixed workload
asi.result: confirmed across 3 keep runs (6 twice, 7 once)
asi.noise_floor: post-refactor noise is +/-1 iter_score on this workload; fw-005 occasionally adds a verify v/cat step
asi.next_optimization_direction: diminishing returns on this fixed workload. Recommend either (a) switch to harder workload (deferred 4Clojure Medium tasks) or (b) widen workload via revalidate.sh rotation. Per autoresearch.md anti-overfit guidance, rotation is the safer next step.

description: Foundation v/ editing prompt: compressed + new explicit "v/patch returns diff+post-image; trust it" anti-verify hint. iter_score=6 tied (best). foundation_prompt_chars 1997→1790 (-10.4%); ext_prompt_chars 5232→5025 (-4%); context_score 6885→6678 (-3%); wall 49→48s (fastest run). pass 3/3, block_errors 0. Primary unchanged at the workload floor; significant secondary improvements + anti-verify guidance that should help fw-005's occasional 3-iter verify step.

asi.hypothesis: shrink foundation prompt + add anti-verify hint
asi.note: kept despite tied primary because secondaries improved across the board AND anti-verify hint should reduce fw-005's 3-iter variance. Reverting on tied would lose the foundation shrink.
next_targets: nothing obvious left on this workload at the floor
candidate: broader/harder workload to expose more iteration waste

description: LOOP DISCIPLINE update: "for trivial tasks tool call IS the evidence". iter_score=6 tied. Wall fastest yet (46.53s) but system_prompt_chars +4.2% with no primary improvement. fw-005 still 2 iters this run (was already at floor). Anti-verify hint may be useful but not exercised by current workload. Discard per tied-primary rule.

asi.hypothesis: system-prompt anti-verify hint reduces fw-005's occasional verify-step iter
asi.rollback_reason: primary tied; ext_prompt_chars grew; the anti-verify hint duplicates the foundation prompt's 'trust the patch result' line
asi.observation: we already have anti-verify in foundation editing prompt; duplicating in CORE is redundant

description: 4Clojure prompt cut Restrictions section: iter_score 6→9, block_errors 0→2 (keep-blocker violated). Restrictions section had a useful structural hint (v/cat to read, z/patch to edit) that the model lost.

asi.hypothesis: Restrictions section is redundant with core prompt
asi.rollback_reason: block_errors 0->2 violates +1 keep-blocker; iter_score regressed to 9
asi.next_action_hint: user prompts have task-specific hints that matter; further compression of 4Clojure prompt is fragile

description: Third confirmation at current best. iter_score=6, pass 3/3, block_errors 0, z_patch_share 100%, wall 48.37s, cost $0.017 (lowest yet). All 3 tasks at 2-iter floor. Sequence: 6, 6, 6 across confirmations — floor is stable and reliably hit.

asi.hypothesis: iter_score=6 is consistently reproducible
asi.result: yes; 3 keeps at 6, one transient 7 from fw-005 verify-step variance

structural_changes: preflight final-answer-gate now threads previous-iterations through (was nil -> always rejected)
evidence-prior? tightened: requires real non-error non-answer block in a prior iter, not just `seq prior-iters`
answer-with-extension preflight no longer rejects whole iteration; drops only turn-answer, lets ext-call run -> stops the model's loop where its patch never lands
CORE prompt compressed 3429 -> 2503 chars (-27%) and stripped of extension-specific names (layering refactor)
z/ ext prompt rewritten as STRUCTURAL EDITING manifesto + SCI reader-macro gotcha warning + (z/source) workaround
foundation v/ editing prompt compressed 1997 -> 1790 with explicit 'trust patch result' anti-verify hint
4Clojure user prompt switched to (z/patch + z/source) shape with literal-list example
runner cache fingerprint uses relative path (multiple core.clj no longer collide)

description: Metric parser fix: count z/v_patch_calls from form-result channel entries instead of tool-event trace chunks. The tool-event hook only fires for BARE top-level tool calls; `(do ... (z/patch ...))` wraps were undercounted. Now z_patch_share is honest (66.67% this run = 2 z/patch + 1 v/patch). iter_score=6 unchanged (this is observability, not behavior). pass 3/3, block_errors 0.

asi.hypothesis: metric parser accuracy fix for z/v_patch_calls counting
asi.fix_details: tool-event chunk only fires for bare top-level tool forms; channel array in form-result lists every tool call run inside the form (including those wrapped in `do`). Switched to channel-based counting.
asi.honest_z_patch_share: now reflects all patches incl. wrapped; this run shows 2 z + 1 v = 66.67% (more accurate than the previous 100% which missed v/patch in fw-005)
asi.session_position: at workload floor; remaining work is widening to harder workloads or accepting current state

description: Stability run + ideas.md session wrap-up. iter_score=6 reproduced. All 3 tasks at 2-iter floor. block_errors=1 (within +1 keep-blocker). z_patch_share 66.67% (honest count now). Confirms the final state is robust. Also persists the wrap-up notes (cumulative wins log) to autoresearch.ideas.md.

asi.hypothesis: stability confirmation + wrap-up
asi.result: iter_score=6 reproduced; 6/6 pass on rotated revalidate (iter_score=14 across 6 tasks)
asi.session_close: 31 experiments. baseline 39 -> 6 (-85%). 8 distinct architectural+prompt improvements committed; metric parser fixed. wrap-up notes archived in autoresearch.ideas.md.

description: Final stability sample. iter_score=6, pass 3/3, block_errors=0, wall 50.27s, z_patch_share 66.67%. 4 of last 5 same-code runs hit iter_score=6 (one transient 7). Floor confirmed robust.

asi.hypothesis: iter_score=6 stable across many same-code runs

description: Another stability sample at floor. iter_score=6 reproduced (5 of last 6 runs). pass 3/3. wall 51.86s.

asi.hypothesis: floor stability sampling
